svg.text: Hardware-Aware Blocking: Detection vs Conservative Default
How the kernel decides tile sizes for fused operations
// Always use cache line as minimum unit
2. The Right Approach: Detect Once, Fall Back Gracefully
// system_topology.h - You already have this structure!
// Conservative: 1 cache line per KV element
3. The Fused Pipeline: Processing in Cache-Line Chunks
Whether T=128 (optimal) or T=1 (conservative), the fusion logic is identical - only tile size changes:
Key Insight: Cache Line is the Fundamental Unit
64 bytes = 32 FP16 = 16 FP32 = 1 ZMM register = 1 AVX-512 load
Everything aligns to this: tiles, head boundaries, output chunks
Process 64B through entire fusion → GUARANTEED no false sharing
Optimal (T=128): 1 iteration → max throughput, needs detection
Conservative (T=1): 128 iterations → loop overhead, always works
In practice: detection overhead is ~1μs, amortized over millions of ops
// The universal fused kernel - tile size is a parameter, not hardcoded
// ... rest of kernel uses T_kv, T_gemm for all blocking decisions

g.text: Just need to wire it to kernel tile decisions!

// Process entire transformer block in cache-line aligned chunks

qkv_gemm_fused(Q, K, V, x_norm, W_qkv, b_qkv, T_gemm);
O = flash_attention(Q, K, V, kv_cache, T_kv);
